heading: Imposition of tax
label: 2801 Imposition of tax
notes: Amendments2014—Subsec. (a)(1). struck out “(or, if greater, the highest rate of tax specified in the table applicable under section 2502(a) as in effect on the date)” after “such receipt”.Pub. L. 113–295

L. 110–245, title III, § 301(g)122 Stat. 1647June 17, 2008“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [enacting this chapter and and amending sections 877, 6039G, and 7701 of this title] shall apply to any individual whose expatriation date (as so defined) is on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 877A of this titleJune 17, 2008“(2).—Gifts and bequestsChapter 15 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by subsection (b)) shall apply to covered gifts and bequests (as defined in section 2801 of such Code, as so added) received on or after the date of the enactment of this Act from transferors (or from the estates of transferors) whose expatriation date is on or after such date of enactment.”

children.text: the highest rate of tax specified in the table contained in section 2001(c) as in effect on the date of such receipt, and
the value of such covered gift or bequest.

children.heading: Tax to be paid by recipient
children.label: b Tax to be paid by recipient
children.text: The tax imposed by subsection (a) on any covered gift or bequest shall be paid by the person receiving such gift or bequest.

children.heading: Exception for certain gifts
children.label: c Exception for certain gifts
children.text: Subsection (a) shall apply only to the extent that the value of covered gifts and bequests received by any person during the calendar year exceeds the dollar amount in effect under section 2503(b) for such calendar year.

children.heading: Tax reduced by foreign gift or estate tax
children.label: d Tax reduced by foreign gift or estate tax
children.text: The tax imposed by subsection (a) on any covered gift or bequest shall be reduced by the amount of any gift or estate tax paid to a foreign country with respect to such covered gift or bequest.

children.label: A
children.text: any property acquired by gift directly or indirectly from an individual who, at the time of such acquisition, is a covered expatriate, and

children.label: B
children.text: any property acquired directly or indirectly by reason of the death of an individual who, immediately before such death, was a covered expatriate.

children.heading: Exceptions for transfers otherwise subject to estate or gift tax
children.label: 2 Exceptions for transfers otherwise subject to estate or gift tax
A
children.text: any property shown on a timely filed return of tax imposed by chapter 12 which is a taxable gift by the covered expatriate, and

children.label: B
children.text: any property included in the gross estate of the covered expatriate for purposes of chapter 11 and shown on a timely filed return of tax imposed by chapter 11 of the estate of the covered expatriate.

children.heading: Exceptions for transfers to spouse or charity
children.label: 3 Exceptions for transfers to spouse or charity
children.text: Such term shall not include any property with respect to which a deduction would be allowed under section 2055, 2056, 2522, or 2523, whichever is appropriate, if the decedent or donor were a United States person.

children.label: i
children.text: subsection (a) shall apply in the same manner as if such trust were a United States citizen, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the tax imposed by subsection (a) on such gift or bequest shall be paid by such trust.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a covered gift or bequest made to a foreign trust, subsection (a) shall apply to any distribution attributable to such gift or bequest from such trust (whether from income or corpus) to a United States citizen or resident in the same manner as if such distribution were a covered gift or bequest.

children.heading: Deduction for tax paid by recipient
children.label: ii Deduction for tax paid by recipient
children.text: There shall be allowed as a deduction under section 164 the amount of tax imposed by this section which is paid or accrued by a United States citizen or resident by reason of a distribution from a foreign trust, but only to the extent such tax is imposed on the portion of such distribution which is included in the gross income of such citizen or resident.

children.heading: Election to be treated as domestic trust
children.label: iii Election to be treated as domestic trust
children.text: Solely for purposes of this section, a foreign trust may elect to be treated as a domestic trust. Such an election may be revoked with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Covered expatriate
children.label: f Covered expatriate
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “covered expatriate” has the meaning given to such term by section 877A(g)(1).
